question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the /api/v1/accounts/{id}/rewards endpoint by supplying an EVM address or alias that resolves to a different account than intended via EntityService.getEncodedId(), causing the staking reward transfer history of the wrong account to be returned, leaking reward data and potentially enabling reward theft through account confusion?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isValidAccount() check in listStakingRewardsByAccountId() — which throws NotFoundError if the account does not exist — by observing that a deleted account (deleted=true) may still pass the isValidAccount() check if the entity table retains the row, allowing the attacker to query staking reward history for deleted accounts and observe rewards that were never paid out?
